title: Machine learning bioactivity prediction from BGCs
key_findings: Machine learning models can predict natural product bioactivity from BGC sequences
BGC genomic features contain bioactivity information
Prediction enables prioritization of BGCs before experimental production
Method addresses major bottleneck in drug discovery pipeline

bioactivity_prediction_performance.model_accuracy: High correlation between predicted and actual bioactivity
bioactivity_prediction_performance.prediction_scope: Extends beyond antibiotic activity to multiple bioactivity types
bioactivity_prediction_performance.improvement_over_existing_methods: Resistance marker methods (RGI, ARTS) only identify antibacterial compounds

historical_context.fda_approved_natural_products: 23.5% small molecule NPs, 22.5% synthetics mimicking NPs
historical_context.total_influence: 46% of FDA-approved drugs derive from natural products
historical_context.research_gap: Many structurally fascinating molecules still lack identified functions

efficiency_improvement.throughput_increase: Focuses resources on most promising BGCs
efficiency_improvement.cost_reduction: Avoids unnecessary production and assaying of low-potential compounds

bgc_prioritization_framework.approach: Rank BGCs by predicted bioactivity probability
bgc_prioritization_framework.benefit: Researchers can focus on BGCs most likely to produce desired bioactivities
bgc_prioritization_framework.application: Accelerates discovery of antibiotics, anticancer, antifungal compounds

discovery_challenges_addressed: Identifying bioactive compounds among 147,000+ known BGCs

resistance_marker_approach.mechanism: Identifies resistance markers or gene duplication

methodology: Bioactivity testing against multiple pathogen panels
Comparison of predicted vs experimental bioactivity
success_metrics: Cost and time savings vs traditional screening

ml_enhanced_workflow: Selective production of predicted bioactive compounds

bioactivity_categories_predicted.antibacterial: Extends beyond existing resistance marker methods

research_implications: BGC sequences contain exploitable bioactivity information
Machine learning enables knowledge extraction from genomic data
Prediction-based prioritization accelerates natural product discovery
Reduces cost and time of drug discovery pipeline
Enables discovery of compounds with diverse bioactivities
future_applications: Integration with other omics data for improved prediction
